summary: Vector core exception came from using outputHw-sized channel slices as UB vector inputs, which made per-channel starts unaligned. Fix by padding each channel buffer to block alignment, indexing channels with alignedOutputHw, and computing group statistics with scalar loops instead of vector reduction on misaligned packed buffers.

Large global output mismatch was caused by implementing sigmoid as x/(1+exp(-x)), which is swish, not sigmoid. Replace the manual exp/div chain with AscendC::Sigmoid or compute 1/(1+exp(-x)) explicitly, then keep the normalized path unchanged.

When scalar group-mean/group-variance logic consumes tensors written by vector ops, add explicit V_S and S_V synchronization around the scalar/vector boundary. Without those flags the kernel may run correctly but compute stale statistics and produce widespread numeric mismatch.

Residual errors only at each group’s first channel first element came from reusing groupLocal as a temporary scalar sqrt buffer, which overwrote live activation data. Use a dedicated scratch tensor such as tmpLocal for scalar variance/std computation instead of writing into the data buffer being normalized.
